nodes.summary: Implemented a shared workspace settings system so the settings modal now controls real UI behavior across the app. The settings state was lifted to the page and threaded into the sidebar, artifact viewer, command center, and modal. Theme now updates the overall shell and panel styling, density changes spacing, font size adjusts artifact text/table rendering, line numbers can be toggled in the viewer, and auto-scroll can be disabled in the chat panel. The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.

affectedFiles.reason: Lifted settings state to the workspace page and passed shared settings into the sidebar, artifact viewer, and command center.
affectedFiles.notes: Also updated the mobile shell to reflect theme changes.

affectedFiles.reason: Converted modal controls to receive shared settings state and dispatch updates instead of maintaining isolated local state.
affectedFiles.notes: The modal now reflects the app theme and density state.

affectedFiles.reason: Sidebar now passes the shared settings state into the modal so theme changes and control updates can affect the whole app.
affectedFiles.notes: The settings button still opens the modal.

affectedFiles.reason: Artifact rendering now responds to shared line-number, font-size, theme, and density settings.
affectedFiles.notes: Text and sheet rendering both use the shared settings contract.

affectedFiles.reason: Chat panel now respects shared theme, density, and auto-scroll settings.
affectedFiles.notes: Auto-scroll can be toggled off from the modal.

affectedFiles.reason: Introduced a shared typed settings contract to avoid duplicating setting shapes and to keep state updates explicit.
affectedFiles.notes: Defines the default workspace settings and updater type.

nodes.summary: Adjusted the chat UI layout so the command center message list scrolls independently and the composer stays attached to the bottom without a separator line. The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

affectedFiles.reason: Contains the message list and composer layout; updated to make the message list scrollable and remove the separator above the composer.
affectedFiles.notes: Added flex sizing and overflow handling to the message list wrapper and removed the composer top border.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the chat panel container whose flex sizing must allow the internal scroll area to shrink properly.
affectedFiles.notes: Added min-h-0 to the chat panel wrapper and its sibling split panel wrapper.

nodes.summary: Implemented a Supabase-backed workspace and chat flow. The app now loads real workspaces from Supabase, allows creating a workspace through a modal opened from a plus button in the sidebar and mobile header, and loads workspace-specific chat history through a workspace chat API. The chat send path now requires a valid workspace, validates that conversations belong to the selected workspace, and falls back to creating a conversation inside that workspace when needed. No SQL migration changes were required because the existing workspaces/conversations/messages schema already supported the flow.

affectedFiles.reason: Replaced mock workspace state with Supabase-loaded workspaces, added workspace creation flow, and wired the page to the new chat loader and modal.
affectedFiles.notes: Main UI orchestrator for the feature.

affectedFiles.reason: Sidebar now shows real workspace rows, adds a plus button for creating workspaces, and handles the empty-workspace state.
affectedFiles.notes: Desktop navigation entrypoint.

affectedFiles.reason: New modal component for creating workspaces from the sidebar/mobile header CTA with validation and inline error handling.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps workspace creation UI isolated.

affectedFiles.reason: Hook now loads persisted workspace chat history from Supabase before streaming new messages and blocks sends while history is loading.
affectedFiles.notes: Client-side chat state and streaming logic.

affectedFiles.reason: New API route to list workspaces and create a new workspace with validation.
affectedFiles.notes: Server boundary for workspace CRUD.

affectedFiles.reason: New API route to return the latest conversation and persisted messages for a workspace.
affectedFiles.notes: Server boundary for workspace-specific chat loading.

affectedFiles.reason: Chat streaming route now enforces valid workspace ownership for conversations and no longer falls back to the old auto-created default workspace flow.
affectedFiles.notes: Prevents chats from escaping the selected workspace.

affectedFiles.reason: Added a listWorkspaces helper used by the new workspace listing API.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps workspace data access centralized.

affectedFiles.reason: Added workspace conversation listing helpers used by the workspace chat loader.
affectedFiles.notes: Supports selecting the active workspace conversation.

affectedFiles.reason: Added a neutral empty-state panel so the chat area still has a useful workspace-ready state when there are no messages.
affectedFiles.notes: Improves UX for newly created or empty workspaces.

affectedFiles.reason: Verified the existing schema already contained workspaces, conversations, and messages, so no migration was needed for this feature.
affectedFiles.notes: No file changes required.

nodes.summary: User wants the left workspace panel (ArtifactViewer area) to support uploading files, extract text from txt/md/pdf/docx into line-based content, persist the extracted content in Supabase, and leave a clean hook for a future AI file-reading tool. I inspected the workspace page, ArtifactViewer, chat input, workspace repository, Supabase schema/types, and existing API patterns to shape the plan.

affectedFiles.reason: This is the top-level workspace composition and controls the left ArtifactViewer panel where upload support should be integrated.
affectedFiles.notes: Contains current workspace state, sample artifacts, and panel layout.

affectedFiles.reason: This is the workspace panel component that will likely host upload/dropzone and file preview behavior.
affectedFiles.notes: Currently only renders tabs, content, and an empty state CTA.

affectedFiles.reason: This empty state is the most likely UI entry point for adding upload affordances and supported-format guidance.
affectedFiles.notes: Currently only offers a sample deploy button.

affectedFiles.reason: Contains an existing attachment button that may inform or share UX patterns for file input styling.
affectedFiles.notes: No file upload logic exists yet.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the established Supabase repository pattern to mirror for workspace file persistence.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for future repository separation.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the repository structure and validation/error-handling style to follow for new file ingestion helpers.
affectedFiles.notes: Good reference for server-side data access conventions.

affectedFiles.reason: This file will need to be extended to include any new file/document tables introduced by the migration.
affectedFiles.notes: Must stay in sync with Supabase schema.

affectedFiles.reason: Baseline migration shows current tables and trigger/RLS patterns to extend for uploaded files.
affectedFiles.notes: New migration should follow the same style.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the existing API style for workspace list/create routes, which new file routes should match.
affectedFiles.notes: Validates JSON handling and error responses.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the pattern for workspace-scoped API routes that the file ingestion route should mirror.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for params handling and 404 behavior.

nodes.summary: Implemented workspace file upload and text extraction flow. The workspace panel now exposes a real upload button and empty-state CTA, uploads txt/md/pdf/docx through a workspace-scoped API route, extracts normalized line-based text on the server, persists uploaded file metadata and extracted lines in Supabase, and keeps a repository hook ready for future AI file-reading tools.

affectedFiles.reason: Adds the new Supabase table and related indexes/triggers needed to persist uploaded files and extracted lines.
affectedFiles.notes: Includes RLS enablement and updated_at trigger.

affectedFiles.reason: Extends the generated-style database contract with workspace file row, insert, and relationship types.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps client/server type usage explicit.

affectedFiles.reason: Centralizes file-type allowlisting, size limits, and accept-string generation for upload validation.
affectedFiles.notes: Shared by client and server code.

affectedFiles.reason: Implements server-side extraction and normalization for txt, md, pdf, and docx files into line arrays.
affectedFiles.notes: Uses built-in parsing logic rather than external packages.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides Supabase persistence and retrieval helpers for uploaded workspace files, plus a reusable line-range helper for future AI reads.
affectedFiles.notes: Server-side data access only.

affectedFiles.reason: Wraps the workspace file API for the client-side page orchestration layer.
affectedFiles.notes: Handles fetch, upload, and response validation.

affectedFiles.reason: Converts persisted file rows into ArtifactViewer artifacts so uploaded files render with the existing line-based viewer.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps UI mapping separate from data access.

affectedFiles.reason: Exposes workspace-scoped GET and POST endpoints for listing and uploading extracted files.
affectedFiles.notes: Validates workspace existence and multipart uploads.

affectedFiles.reason: Adds the workspace file header upload control to the left panel while keeping the viewer composition-focused.
affectedFiles.notes: Now accepts fileCount and onUploadFiles props.

affectedFiles.reason: Reworks the empty state into a file upload call to action with the supported document formats.
affectedFiles.notes: Removed the old sample deploy wording.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the new top-of-panel upload entry point and file count display without bloating the viewer entry file.
affectedFiles.notes: Uses neutral styling consistent with the app.

affectedFiles.reason: Orchestrates workspace file loading, upload actions, hidden file input handling, and artifact state synchronization.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the page as the composition layer and delegates extraction/persistence to helpers.

nodes.summary: Fixed the workspace file upload failure caused by malformed Unicode reaching Supabase. The ingestion pipeline now sanitizes extracted and inserted text to remove NULs and lone surrogates before persistence, which should prevent the Postgres Unicode escape error during file uploads.

affectedFiles.reason: Added Unicode normalization and sanitization for extracted file text, line arrays, and upload metadata before persistence.
affectedFiles.notes: Removes NUL characters and lone surrogates.

affectedFiles.reason: Added defensive sanitization before the Supabase insert to prevent malformed Unicode from reaching the database.
affectedFiles.notes: Provides a second validation layer at persistence time.

nodes.summary: Switched workspace file ingestion from hand-rolled parsing to dedicated PDF/DOCX libraries. The upload path now targets @cedrugs/pdf-parse for PDFs and mammoth for DOCX, while continuing to normalize extracted text into numbered lines for the viewer and future AI file-reading tool. Added ambient declarations for the new modules and updated package.json dependencies accordingly.

affectedFiles.reason: Replaced the custom PDF/DOCX extraction heuristics with proper parser-based extraction so uploads yield readable text lines.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps sanitization and line normalization after extraction.

affectedFiles.reason: Added the new parser dependencies required for readable PDF and DOCX extraction.
affectedFiles.notes: Dependencies need to be installed into node_modules.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the mammoth parser import used by the ingestion layer.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the PDF parser import used by the ingestion layer.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.

nodes.summary: Updated workspace document ingestion so extracted PDF/DOCX/TXT/MD content now drops leading and trailing blank lines before storing both the numbered line array and the concatenated extracted text. This keeps the downstream contract intact while removing empty boundary lines from uploaded documents.

affectedFiles.reason: Implemented trimming of leading and trailing empty lines during workspace document ingestion and rebuilt stored extracted text from the trimmed lines.
affectedFiles.notes: No other files were changed for this fix.
